steps.title: Set up the exponential model
steps.content:
Doubling each day is exponential growth with base $2$. The general form is $y = a \cdot b^{t}$, where $a$ is the starting value and $b$ is the per-day multiplier.

Here $a = 72{,}000$ and $b = 2$, giving $y = 72{,}000(2)^{t}$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A. $y = \tfrac{1}{2}(72{,}000)^{t}$: at $t=0$ gives $\tfrac{1}{2}$, not $72{,}000$. Wrong start and wrong base.
  • B. $y = 2(72{,}000)^{t}$: at $t=0$ gives $2$, not $72{,}000$. Wrong.
  • C. $y = 72{,}000(\tfrac{1}{2})^{t}$: this halves each day, not doubles. Wrong.
  • D. $y = 72{,}000(2)^{t}$: correct start, correct doubling factor. That matches choice D — D is correct.
